rulings.Case Title: EST.OF ESTELLE MCCLELLAND
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Vacating/Set Aside Judgement Due To Extrinsic Fraud/Mistake
rulings.Rulings: The Motion for Order Vacating/Set Aside Judgment Due to Extrinsic Fraud/Mistake is denied. Even assuming it has been properly noticed on all interested parties, which does not appear from the record, Ms. Sturgell has failed to present credible evidence from which the Court could conclude either that the Public Administrator was required to give her notice of the petition to administer the estate or that the Public Administrator committed any fraud on the court in procuring letters of administration or carrying out his duties as administrator. Ms. Sturgell's entitlement to notice depends on her father being dead when these proceedings were instituted. She has offered only speculation that was the case. Even if he had been dead, Ms. Sturgell has offered no evidence that she was known to or readily ascertainable by the Public Administrator. She points to savings bonds she says she received after her aunt's death, but she offers no proof that they were sent by the Public Administrator or even that the Public Administrator was aware of the aunt's practice of sending bonds to her nieces. She points to a power of attorney by her father in favor of a third party, which she claims was forged, but she offers no proof that the Public Administrator knew of any forgery or participated in any scheme to administer the estate on the basis of such a forgery. There is no evidence of any collusion or conspiracy between the Public Administrator and the alleged perpetrator of the alleged fraud, Mr. Morris. The probate petition was duly published in a newspaper of record, giving notice to unknown heirs in accordance with the Probate Code. The other evidence and arguments offered by Ms. Sturgell are irrelevant or go only to the merits of the estate administration orders and could not provide a basis to vacate the earlier judgments. In sum, Ms. Sturgell's motion provides no basis to disturb the finality of the 1999 order issuing testamentary letters to the Public Administrator or the 2001 order approving final distribution of the estate. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise all other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. Tuesday, July 25, 2017

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WALTER J HARRINGTON
rulings.Calendar Matter: Contestants Paul Harringtons, Mark Harringtons, John Harringtons, And Gloria Grecos Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure 664.6
rulings.Rulings: Contestants' motion is denied in part and granted in part. There is no showing that it was mutually understood and agreed that the successor trustee would sign the requested tax forms (see the e-mail on this subject). Further, there is no clear showing that the contestants are actually entitled to the tax exemption per 563.1 et al. Contestants are entitled to be paid for the hold over rent although it should be adjusted to reflect the sums inadvertently paid post settlement for the mortgage and HOA fees. Since there was no clear prevailing party, each party to bear its own fees and costs. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise all other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. Tuesday, July 25, 2017.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE PATRICIA COATES TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioner Caron Coates Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement
rulings.Rulings: The motion to enforce the settlement is denied. There is no showing of a breach via sale of the San Rafael property since the net proceeds of the sale were not in excess of $765,0000. Further, there is no showing that the wrong wedding ring was delivered, and the CPS report, if in fact instigated by Respondents, was not a part of or related to the settlement agreement. Reasonable attorneys fees, not in excess of $900.00 will be awarded to Respondent Coates along with $428.24 in costs, should he personally appear at the hearing. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise all other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. Tuesday, July 25, 2017
